data_collection_plan: EVENT-FIRST, NOT PROFILE-FIRST: the atomic sellable unit is one immutable check-in row per consumption moment (the Untappd/Vivino 'consumption moment' asset), never a per-user favorites list. Collect from record one; retrofitting a churned base is impossible.

Per check-in event, capture: beer_id + brewery_id + sku_canonical_id (mapped to a PROPRIETARY canonical brand/brewery/style taxonomy that is itself a core moat), style/substyle, abv, ibu, srm, rating 0-5, structured flavor_tags[] + ABSA aspect_scores (appearance/aroma/palate/taste), geo_bucket (H3/ZIP3/DMA - never raw lat/long in the sellable layer), venue_id, on_off_premise flag, event_ts with derived day_of_week/daypart/season, occasion (home/bar/restaurant/event/sports), and purchase-intent (saved, wishlisted, where_to_buy_clicks, price_paid, price_tier).

INSTRUMENT THE CPG SIGNALS OTHER APPS SKIP: daypart/season, on- vs off-premise, occasion/context, and pre-purchase intent. Circana/NIQ already own POS scanner data - what they PAY for is this attitudinal + occasion + intent layer they cannot derive from till data.

Derive per-user preference vectors (top_styles[], abv/ibu comfort bands, novelty_score, price_tier_pref) to power the recommendation flywheel, but keep these on the PERSONAL PLANE - never sold at the individual level.

TWO-PLANE ARCHITECTURE WITH A HARD BOUNDARY. Personal plane (never sold): raw check-ins, exact venue, timestamp, friends, review_text - user-controlled; coarse flavor-tag NLP runs on-device and only anonymized tags leave the phone. Aggregate plane (the only thing sold): server roll-ups keyed geo-bucket x style x rolling-window.

Every event row carries consent metadata (consent_version, sale_optin, location_optin, gpc_flag) so the aggregation pipeline can EXCLUDE any opted-out user BEFORE roll-up - this is what makes the aggregate layer defensible and AB 1824-safe.

Optimize for per-market DENSITY over vanity national DAU: saturate 2-4 launch metros so you can credibly tell a brewery/distributor 'we have statistically meaningful volume in YOUR territory.' Density x exclusivity x contracted B2B revenue x refresh velocity are the valuation levers - not raw DAU.

Maintain a sensitive-location SUPPRESSION LIST from day one (addiction-treatment/AA sites, medical, reproductive, religious, shelters) so those geopoints NEVER enter the sellable layer - the exact FTC hot-button for an alcohol app. Truncate/round precision and cap retention.

Aggregate gates at query time: k-anonymity k>=10 distinct users AND a minimum distinct-venue count, minimum-sample suppression (sparse geos/weeks auto-roll-up), and calibrated differential-privacy noise on published counts/shares.

Two-stage flavor/sentiment NLP: Stage 1 fine-tuned encoder does aspect-based sentiment analysis (BeerAdvocate 4-aspect schema) on-device; Stage 2 server-side LLM pass handles negation/sarcasm/slang on already-de-identified text. Aggregate sentiment only at geo x style x window under the same k-threshold.
apple_maps_plan: BUILD NATIVE SWIFT/SwiftUI - the map is the signature surface and the live B2B demo, and its three differentiators (dense clustering, a real check-in heatmap, Apple Place Card + Look Around wired to our tap data) are exactly what expo-maps and react-native-maps CANNOT do. This is the decisive input to the stack decision: go native.

SwiftUI Map is the default; drop to a surgical UIViewRepresentable(MKMapView) ONLY for the two things SwiftUI still lacks - high-density clustering and the true MKOverlayRenderer heatmap. Keep ~90% of the screen idiomatic SwiftUI (Marker/Annotation/selection/camera).

Base map: Map(position:selection:) with .mapStyle(.standard(pointsOfInterest: .including([.brewery,.bar,.nightlife,.winery,.distillery]))) so even Apple's own basemap POIs are constrained to beer venues.

Nearby search: MKLocalPointsOfInterestRequest(center:radius<=25km) + the POI-category filter - purpose-built for 'breweries near me' with no natural-language query. MKLocalSearch.Request with naturalLanguageQuery is the search-bar fallback. Debounce on .onMapCameraChange(frequency:.onEnd) and cache by region tile because MKLocalSearch is rate-limited.

Catalog <-> Apple join: persist MKMapItem.identifier (iOS 18) on brewery rows; when only address/coords exist, resolve via GeoToolbox PlaceDescriptor + MKMapItemRequest (iOS 26); fallback dedupe = proximity (<~30m) + normalized name. OUR catalog is the source of truth (Apple undercounts/miscategorizes small taprooms); Apple is the enrichment layer.
Ownership split: Apple owns geometry/hours/photos/Look Around/Place Card; WE own taplist, styles/IBU, live check-in density, verified-partner flag, offers.

Callout: native Place Card via .mapItemDetailSelectionAccessory(.callout/.sheet) (iOS 18) for hours/photos/directions, plus an injected custom section (current taps, style/IBU chips, live check-in count, and a Look Around thumbnail GATED on a non-nil MKLookAroundScene - coverage is city-limited, degrade gracefully).

Clustering: SwiftUI Map + an off-main-thread QuadTree pre-clusterer (ClusterMap-style) above a few hundred pins; UIKit escape hatch (clusteringIdentifier + MKMarkerAnnotationView) for native cluster animation.

Heatmap ('hot now'): server pre-aggregates check-ins into H3 cells; SwiftUI path draws one MapCircle per hot cell (opacity proportional to density); true-gradient path uses a UIKit MKOverlay + custom MKOverlayRenderer.

Target iOS 18 minimum (iOS 26 preferred) for mapItemDetailSelectionAccessory, MKMapItem.identifier, GeoToolbox and the newest POI/Look Around behavior. Migrate off deprecated CLGeocoder/CLPlacemark to MKGeocodingRequest/MKReverseGeocodingRequest and off pre-iOS-17 MapAnnotation/MapMarker.
privacy_consent: THE COMPANY SALE (M&A) IS THE CLEAN PATH; the ongoing DATA SALE is the high-risk one - both are enabled by the same day-one disclosures. The existential risk is the FTC (not the App Store or CCPA): an alcohol app selling precise location is squarely in the 2024-2026 location-data-broker enforcement lane and the FTC has BANNED companies from selling sensitive location outright.

Ship a counsel-drafted Privacy Policy BUSINESS-TRANSFER clause: personal data may be transferred as a business asset in a merger, acquisition, financing, reorganization, bankruptcy, or asset sale, and the successor may keep processing under this policy.
Ship a ToS ASSIGNMENT clause: we may assign the agreement to a successor/acquirer WITHOUT user consent.

DUAL DISCLOSURE in notice-at-collection: data may be SOLD/SHARED with third parties for stated purposes AND TRANSFERRED as an M&A asset - this dual disclosure keeps the company sale inside the CCPA business-transfer exemption and gives GDPR transparency cover.

NEVER include an absolute 'we will never sell or transfer your data' line - it legally forecloses the exit and cannot be fixed retroactively on a churned user base. Purpose language must pre-authorize aggregated/anonymized third-party analytics + data-clean-room use.

Layered, UNBUNDLED, default-OFF consent capture: neutral pre-permission explainer -> iOS When-In-Use location prompt (request Always only with a genuine background feature) -> separate opt-in toggles for 'share/sell my data with partners' and 'use my precise location for [named purpose]' -> the ATT system prompt with an honest NSUserTrackingUsageDescription. Selling to data brokers IS 'tracking' under Apple's definition, so ATT is mandatory; never gate features on ATT or bribe consent (5.1.2(i)).

DURABLE CONSENT + OPT-OUT LEDGER from record one: timestamped, versioned, per-purpose, storing the exact UI text shown, EXPORTABLE to a buyer, including every CCPA do-not-sell/share and honored GPC signal. AB 1824 (in force Jan 1 2025) makes an acquirer inherit and honor every pre-close opt-out (penalties $2,500-$7,500/violation) - a clean exportable ledger is a diligence pass/fail that moves the price.

Apple compliance: age-rate 18+ under the 2025 bands (17+ no longer exists) + first-launch DOB gate, keeping the DOB OUT of the saleable dataset. File Privacy Nutrition Labels declaring Location + Preferences/Purchases under BOTH 'Data Linked to You' AND 'Data Used to Track You'; ship a PrivacyInfo.xcprivacy manifest; audit every third-party SDK (their tracking is your violation).

U.S. state law: publish 'Do Not Sell or Share' + 'Limit the Use of My Sensitive PI' links, honor GPC automatically, classify precise geolocation as SPI, ASSUME you are a California data broker (calendar Jan 1-31 CPPA registration ~$6,600, wire DROP bulk-deletion live Aug 1 2026 on a 45-day cycle, register in TX/OR/VT too), and run the CCPA risk assessment required before selling/sharing or processing SPI as of Jan 1 2026.

GDPR/EU: selling data and monetizing location need freely-given, unbundled, withdrawable OPT-IN - legitimate interest will not hold; no tracking walls; fire no non-essential SDK before consent; GDPR anonymization must be IRREVERSIBLE (higher bar than CCPA de-identification). Keep the EU dataset ARCHITECTURALLY SEPARABLE so it can be excluded from a data sale a buyer isn't GDPR-comfortable with.

DEFAULT COMMERCIAL PRODUCT = the aggregated, anonymized layer delivered via reports/dashboards/clean rooms, NEVER raw PII (the Untappd/Vivino model) - it minimizes 'sale of personal information' exposure, carries the highest margin, and is the format acquirers actually want.
taste_graph_fields: checkin_event.user_id uuid FK -> user_profile (personal plane join key; never in exports)
checkin_event.sku_canonical_id uuid FK -> sku_taxonomy (proprietary join key to CPG systems)
checkin_event.geo_bucket_h3 text (H3 res-8/9 cell; NO raw lat/long in sellable layer)
checkin_event.occasion occasion_kind (enum: home, bar, restaurant, event, sports)
checkin_event.event_ts timestamptz + generated day_of_week smallint, daypart daypart_kind, season season_kind
checkin_event.consent_version text + sale_optin bool + location_optin bool + gpc_flag bool (row-level consent provenance)
checkin_review.aspect_scores jsonb (ABSA appearance/aroma/palate/taste/overall; personal plane; raw review_text stays on-device)

taste_vector.user_id uuid PK + top_styles text[] + abv_comfort_band numrange + ibu_comfort_band int4range + novelty_score real + price_tier_pref price_tier_kind (derived; personal plane only, never sold)
schema_additions: ENUM TYPES (mirror Lore house style): create type on_off_premise as enum ('on_premise','off_premise'); create type occasion_kind as enum ('home','bar','restaurant','event','sports','other'); create type daypart_kind as enum ('morning','afternoon','evening','late_night'); create type season_kind as enum ('winter','spring','summer','fall'); create type price_tier_kind as enum ('value','mid','premium','luxury'); create type consent_purpose as enum ('functional','analytics_aggregate','sale_share','precise_location'); create type consent_action as enum ('grant','withdraw','gpc_optout','dns_optout');

TABLE sku_taxonomy (the proprietary moat): id uuid PK, canonical_name text, brand text, sub_brand text, style text, substyle text, abv numeric(4,2), ibu smallint, srm smallint, apple_map_item_id text null, created_at/updated_at timestamptz + touch trigger. This is the join key to CPG systems - competitors must rebuild it.

TABLE brewery: id uuid PK, name text, sku_taxonomy_brand_ref, apple_map_item_id text null (MKMapItem.identifier for the Apple join), home_geo_bucket_h3 text, verified_partner bool default false, created_at/updated_at + touch trigger.

TABLE venue: id uuid PK, name text, apple_map_item_id text null, geo_bucket_h3 text not null, on_off_premise on_off_premise, poi_category text (Apple MKPointOfInterestCategory), lat/lng numeric (PERSONAL PLANE precision, RLS-locked, never exported), created_at/updated_at + touch trigger.

TABLE checkin_event (event-first spine, immutable): id uuid PK, user_id uuid FK -> user_profile on delete cascade, beer_id uuid FK -> beer_catalog, brewery_id uuid FK -> brewery, sku_canonical_id uuid FK -> sku_taxonomy, style text, substyle text, abv numeric(4,2), ibu smallint, srm smallint, rating numeric(2,1) check (rating between 0 and 5), flavor_tags text[], geo_bucket_h3 text not null, venue_id uuid FK -> venue, on_off_premise on_off_premise, occasion occasion_kind, price_paid numeric(6,2), price_tier price_tier_kind, purchase_intent_flags jsonb default '{}', event_ts timestamptz not null default now(), day_of_week smallint generated always as (extract(dow from event_ts)) stored, daypart daypart_kind, season season_kind, consent_version text not null, sale_optin bool not null default false, location_optin bool not null default false, gpc_flag bool not null default false, created_at timestamptz default now(). RLS: owner-only read/write on the personal plane.

TABLE checkin_review (personal plane; raw text never leaves device-derived): checkin_id uuid FK -> checkin_event, user_id uuid, aspect_scores jsonb (ABSA), flavor_ontology_tags text[], sentiment_confidence real, created_at. RLS owner-only.

TABLE taste_vector (derived, personal plane, never sold): user_id uuid PK -> user_profile on delete cascade, top_styles text[], abv_comfort_band numrange, ibu_comfort_band int4range, novelty_score real, price_tier_pref price_tier_kind, refreshed_at timestamptz. Service-role maintained.

TABLE consent_ledger (the gating asset - durable, exportable, append-only): id uuid PK, user_id uuid FK -> user_profile on delete cascade, purpose consent_purpose not null, action consent_action not null, granted bool not null, policy_version text not null, ui_text_shown text not null, source text (e.g. 'onboarding_toggle','ATT_prompt','GPC_signal'), region_code text, created_at timestamptz not null default now(). NO update/delete (immutable audit trail); indexed on (user_id, purpose, created_at desc); an Edge Function exports the current-state view per user for M&A diligence and AB 1824.

TABLE sensitive_location_suppression: id uuid PK, geo_bucket_h3 text, category text (addiction_treatment/medical/reproductive/religious/shelter), source text, created_at. Queried at aggregation time to EXCLUDE these geopoints from any sellable roll-up.

MATERIALIZED VIEW / TABLE aggregate_cell (the ONLY thing sold): geo_bucket text, style text, window_start/window_end, distinct_users int check (distinct_users >= 10), distinct_venues int, checkin_count int, style_share numeric, momentum numeric, flavor_sentiment_score numeric, dp_noise_applied bool. Populated by a service-role Edge Function that enforces k-anonymity (k>=10 users AND min venues), minimum-sample suppression (auto-roll-up sparse geos/weeks), differential-privacy noise, sensitive-location exclusion, and drops any row where a contributing user has a matching sale_share opt-out or GPC flag in consent_ledger. No raw PII columns permitted.

REGION SCOPING: add region_code / is_eu_user on user_profile so the EU dataset is architecturally separable and excludable from any data sale (GDPR). Add a lore-style security-definer helper (e.g. taste_sale_eligible(user_id)) so the aggregate pipeline reads consent state without tripping RLS recursion, mirroring the existing lore_trust_tier() pattern.
acquisition_value: THREE REAL ACQUIRER CLASSES, all with live rationales: (1) vertical roll-ups / strategic operators (Next Glass bought Untappd 2016 + BeerAdvocate assets 2020, then took a Providence Strategic Growth investment - owns the demand-side beer dataset + Untappd for Business ~20k venues at $899-$1,199/yr + Untappd Insights); (2) Big Beer / CPG for first-party data (AB InBev's BEES did $52.5B GMV in 2025 with a CDP holding ~100M records - brewery analytics is now competitive weaponry); (3) loyalty/payments/martech (Mastercard bought SessionM 2019 to add a consumer-engagement data layer). A market-research buyer (Circana/NIQ) is plausible for a complementary attitudinal/occasion layer scanner data can't produce.

VALUATION LEVERS RANKED: per-market data density > exclusivity/proprietary SKU taxonomy > contracted recurring B2B revenue > refresh velocity > raw DAU. M&A guidance is explicit that contracted recurring revenue and geographic exclusivity/market position are the most consistent multiple-expansion factors.

SELL AN ASSET PLUS A REVENUE STREAM, not just a dataset: stand up an Untappd-Insights-style B2B product early (Tier 1 Regional Trend Reports, Tier 2 Competitive & Distribution Intelligence, Tier 3 Custom/Clean-Room) off the SAME aggregation engine that powers the consumer maps. Recurring contracts are the single biggest documented multiple-expansion lever.

DATA DECAYS ~22-28%/yr, so demonstrable refresh velocity (a live check-in flow) is a valuation input - a stale one-time dump is worth far less than a live sensor network. A large-but-shallow/inactive base decays toward near-worthlessness.

M&A-READINESS QUARTERLY SCORECARD a Next-Glass- or Circana-type buyer will underwrite: (1) check-ins/day + 90-day retention, (2) per-market density in target metros, (3) B2B ARR + logo count, (4) refresh velocity, (5) consent-ledger completeness (a diligence pass/fail under AB 1824).

CONSENT ARCHITECTURE IS A GATING ASSET: get the legal stack wrong (absolute 'never sell/transfer' line, missing business-transfer/assignment clause, incomplete/non-exportable opt-out ledger) and even a dense, valuable dataset becomes legally UNSELLABLE - and it cannot be fixed retroactively on a churned base.
territory_intelligence: THE MARKET IS PROVEN, NOT HYPOTHETICAL: Untappd Insights already sells this exact territory asset (style/flavor trend monitoring, social sentiment, location-based consumption) as 'aggregated, anonymized, privacy compliant' off 10M+ users and 1B+ consumption moments; in 2025 users checked into 1.5M unique beverages across 2.6M locations. This is a competitive product spec, not a green-field bet.

CONSUMER FEATURE = geo-native discovery, map as the home surface: Local Leaderboards (adjustable radius), 'What's Hot Near You' (ranks by 7/30-day check-in VELOCITY and rating-trend, not all-time volume, so it surfaces genuinely rising local beers instead of re-showing national hype everywhere), Regional Taste Maps (choropleth over metro/DMA/ZIP3/H3 with a style toggle), Trending Styles by City (week-over-week style-share), and Brewery Popularity by Area (local reputation decoupled from national scale).

NAMED, CITABLE INDICES published with sample size n and window (sub-threshold cells suppressed): Style Demand Index (local share indexed to national=100), Momentum (period-over-period delta - the 'up 30%' figure), Flavor Sentiment Score (ABSA net positivity per attribute per region), Local Favorite Rank (rating x local velocity, PII-safe).

2026 DEMAND SIGNALS ARE SALABLE NOW: hazy IPA palate fatigue + West Coast IPA revival, Session Hazy/Hazy Pale growth (4-5.5% ABV), broad lager resurgence, N/A beer ~33.7% YoY on-premise growth; U.S. craft production -4% to ~22.0M bbl in 2025 even as volume share ticked to 13.4%, with hyperlocal small breweries the growth pocket.

TWO-STAGE NLP over the BeerAdvocate 4-aspect schema (appearance/aroma/palate/taste + overall): Stage 1 fine-tuned encoder does aspect-based sentiment analysis tagged to a beer flavor ontology (malt/hop/yeast/wild/off-flavor) runnable on-device; Stage 2 server-side LLM pass handles negation/sarcasm/slang normalization on de-identified text. Aggregate sentiment only at geo x style x window.

PRIVACY IS A SOLVED ENGINEERING PROBLEM AND A FEATURE TO SELL: k-anonymity k>=10 is the accepted floor (k=10 cut max re-identification risk from 100% to ~1.09%), plus a minimum distinct-venue count so one dominant bar can't be de-anonymized, minimum-sample suppression that auto-rolls-up sparse geos/windows, and differential-privacy noise on published aggregates - exactly as 2025 CPG data clean rooms do. This lets the product answer 'what's hot in Austin' without ever exposing that a specific user drinks a specific beer at a specific bar.

SAMPLE-BIAS GUARDRAIL: check-in users skew toward enthusiasts and hazy/hype styles, so 'regional demand' is demand among LOGGERS, not the whole market. Frame outputs as directional momentum/sentiment (not absolute market share), disclose the check-in basis, and calibrate against external anchors (Brewers Association production, distributor depletion) in clean-room engagements.

INCUMBENT RISK: Untappd/Next Glass owns the category with 1B+ moments and existing brewery relationships; thin check-in volume produces noisy, easily-suppressed regional cells. Win a specific territory/niche DENSELY first (hyperlocal depth beats thin national breadth) before promising nationwide demand intelligence.
synthesis.stack_implication: "Go native Swift/SwiftUI (iOS 18 minimum, iOS 26 preferred) - the 'breweries near you' map is the decisive input to the stack decision. The feature's three differentiators - high-density clustering, a true check-in heatmap (MKOverlayRenderer), and Apple's Place Card + Look Around wired to our own tap data - are precisely the APIs that expo-maps (SwiftUI-Map-based, iOS-only, alpha, no clustering, no custom marker views, no UIKit escape hatch) and react-native-maps (confirmed New-Architecture/Fabric custom-marker bugs on Apple Maps, no MKLocalPointsOfInterestRequest category search, no iOS 18 Place Card, no Look Around, no path to a custom heatmap) cannot deliver. Neither wrapper reaches the UIKit MKMapView layer our clustering + heatmap need, and if cross-platform were mandated we would end up writing a native module wrapping our own MKMapView regardless - so there is no cross-platform saving on the one surface that matters most. This aligns with Lore's existing locked decision to build lore-ios as native Swift/ARKit (NOT Expo) and to lean on Apple Maps/MapKit as the base with our card+detail as the IP layer; the beer map is the same architecture pattern. Reuse Lore's H3-as-text-at-the-write-layer convention (no h3-pg on Supabase) for the heatmap cell pre-aggregation, and mirror its RLS-locked personal-plane / service-role-aggregate split."

# Addendum — Taste Graph, Territory Intelligence & Maps

*The sellable asset is not a user's list of liked beers — it is a live, geo/time-stamped consumption-event panel plus a proprietary SKU taxonomy, wrapped (eventually) in a contracted B2B analytics revenue stream. Untappd Insights and Vivino both monetize exactly this. Build to that shape from record one, and ship the transfer-enabling legal stack at launch — a dense dataset with the wrong consent architecture is legally unsellable.*

---

## 1. Design principle: event-first, not profile-first

Every strategic decision below flows from one rule: **the atomic unit is the check-in event, not the user.** One immutable row per consumption moment. The moat is (a) hard-to-replicate longitudinal *per-market density* and (b) a proprietary canonical SKU/brewery/style taxonomy competitors would have to rebuild. Raw DAU is a vanity metric; acquirers underwrite **data density × exclusivity × contracted recurring B2B revenue × refresh velocity.** Behavioral data decays ~22–28%/yr, so a live check-in flow — not a static dump — is what holds value.

Two hard architectural boundaries make this both sellable *and* legally clean:

- **Personal plane (never sold):** raw check-ins, exact venue, timestamp, friends, personal history — user-controlled. Coarse flavor-tag NLP runs on-device; only anonymized structured tags leave the phone, never raw review text.
- **Aggregate plane (the only thing sold):** server roll-ups keyed by `geo-bucket × style × rolling-window`, gated at query time by k-anonymity (k ≥ 10 distinct users *and* a minimum distinct-venue count), minimum-sample suppression (sparse geos/weeks auto-roll-up), and calibrated differential-privacy noise. Geo always snaps to metro/DMA/ZIP3/H3-hex — never raw lat/long.

---

## 2. Data collected from day one

Instrument the high-value CPG signals most apps skip — **daypart/season, on- vs off-premise, occasion, and pre-purchase intent** — because that attitudinal + occasion + intent layer is precisely what scanner-data buyers (Circana/NIQ) cannot derive from POS and will pay for.

| Field group | Concrete fields | Why it's collected (buyer value / product value) | Consent tier |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Beer identity (canonical)** | `beer_id`, `brewery_id`, `style`, `substyle`, `abv`, `ibu`, `srm`, `sku_canonical_id` | The SKU-mapping taxonomy is itself the moat — it makes our data joinable to CPG systems. | Functional (no sale needed) |
| **Rating & sentiment** | `rating` (0–5), `flavor_tags[]` (hoppy/malty/sour/fruity/roasty…), `aspect_scores` (appearance/aroma/palate/taste ABSA), `review_text` (personal plane only) | Drives recs flywheel + the Flavor Sentiment Score sold per region. | Functional; aggregate sold only de-identified |
| **Geo & venue** | `geo_bucket` (H3/ZIP3/DMA), `venue_id`, `on_off_premise` flag, `region_code` | Per-market density = the #1 valuation lever. On/off-premise split is high-value to distributors. | **Precise location = SPI → affirmative opt-in** |
| **Time** | `event_ts`, derived `day_of_week`, `daypart`, `season` | Daypart/season occasion signals command CPG premiums. | Functional |
| **Occasion / context** | `occasion` (home/bar/restaurant/event/sports), `companions_bucket` | The attitudinal layer POS data lacks. | Functional |
| **Purchase intent** | `saved`, `wishlisted`, `where_to_buy_clicks`, `price_paid`, `price_tier` | Pre-purchase intent — closed-loop "does buzz predict sell-through." | Functional; opt-in if tied to identity resale |
| **Derived user vectors** | `top_styles[]`, `abv_comfort_band`, `ibu_comfort_band`, `novelty_score`, `price_tier_pref` | Powers recs; never sold at individual level. | Personal plane only |
| **Consent metadata (every row)** | `consent_version`, `sale_optin`, `location_optin`, `gpc_flag` | Row-level provenance so the aggregate pipeline can exclude any opted-out user *before* roll-up. | — |

**Suppression list (day one, non-negotiable):** maintain a sensitive-location exclusion set (addiction-treatment/AA sites, medical, reproductive, religious, shelters). Those geopoints are the exact FTC hot-button for an alcohol app and must never enter the aggregate/sellable layer. Truncate/round precision and cap retention.

---

## 3. Maps: "breweries near you" (build native Swift/SwiftUI)

The map is the product's signature surface *and* the live demo that sells the B2B product. Three of its differentiators — dense clustering, a real check-in heatmap, and Apple's Place Card + Look Around wired to our own tap data — are exactly what expo-maps and react-native-maps cannot do today. **This is the decisive input to the Swift-vs-Expo stack decision: go native.** If cross-platform were mandated we'd end up writing a native module wrapping our own `MKMapView` anyway — so there is no cross-platform saving on the one surface that matters most. (This also matches the existing native-Swift/ARKit decision and the "Apple Maps as base, our card+detail as the IP layer" direction already locked for the iOS app.)

**Stack decision → native Swift/SwiftUI, iOS 18 minimum (iOS 26 preferred).** SwiftUI `Map` is the default; drop to a surgical `UIViewRepresentable(MKMapView)` only for the two things SwiftUI still can't do: high-density clustering and the true `MKOverlayRenderer` heatmap.

| Layer | Implementation |
|---|---|
| **Base map** | SwiftUI `Map(position:selection:)` with `.mapStyle(.standard(pointsOfInterest: .including([.brewery,.bar,.nightlife,.winery,.distillery])))` so even Apple's basemap POIs are constrained to beer venues. |
| **Nearby search** | `MKLocalPointsOfInterestRequest(center:radius:)` (≤ 25 km) + POI-category filter — purpose-built for "breweries near me," no natural-language query. `MKLocalSearch.Request` w/ `naturalLanguageQuery` is the search-bar fallback. |
| **Catalog ↔ Apple join** | Persist `MKMapItem.identifier` (iOS 18) on our brewery rows. When we only have address/coords, resolve via GeoToolbox `PlaceDescriptor` + `MKMapItemRequest` (iOS 26); fallback dedupe = proximity (<~30 m) + normalized name. |
| **Ownership split** | Apple owns geometry, hours, photos, Look Around, Place Card. **We** own taplist, styles/IBU, live check-in density, verified-partner flag, offers. |
| **Callout** | Native Place Card via `.mapItemDetailSelectionAccessory(.callout/.sheet)` (iOS 18) for hours/photos/directions, with an injected custom section: current taps, style/IBU chips, live check-in count, and a Look Around thumbnail **gated on a non-nil `MKLookAroundScene`** (coverage is city-limited — degrade gracefully). |
| **Clustering** | SwiftUI Map + an off-main-thread QuadTree pre-clusterer (ClusterMap-style) above a few hundred pins; UIKit escape hatch (`clusteringIdentifier` + `MKMarkerAnnotationView`) if we want Apple's native cluster animation. |
| **Heatmap ("hot now")** | Server pre-aggregates check-ins into H3 cells (H3 computed at the write layer — no `h3-pg` on Supabase, cells stored as `text`, matching house convention). SwiftUI path: one `MapCircle` per hot cell, opacity ∝ density. True-gradient path: UIKit `MKOverlay` + custom `MKOverlayRenderer`. |
| **Performance** | Debounce on `.onMapCameraChange(frequency:.onEnd)`; cache by region tile (Apple rate-limits `MKLocalSearch`); degrade to catalog-only pins when throttled. |

**Deprecations to avoid shipping on:** `CLGeocoder`/`CLPlacemark` (use `MKGeocodingRequest`/`MKReverseGeocodingRequest`) and the pre-iOS-17 `MapAnnotation`/`MapMarker`. Note: our catalog is the source of truth (Apple undercounts small taprooms and miscategorizes them as `.restaurant`/`.bar`); Apple is the enrichment layer, not vice versa.

**Cross-platform verdict (for the record):** `expo-maps` (AppleMaps) is SwiftUI-Map-based, iOS-only, alpha, with no clustering, no custom marker views, and no UIKit escape hatch. `react-native-maps` (`PROVIDER_DEFAULT`) has confirmed New-Architecture/Fabric custom-marker bugs on Apple Maps, does not expose `MKLocalPointsOfInterestRequest` category search, the iOS 18 Place Card, or Look Around, and offers no path to a custom heatmap. Neither reaches the UIKit `MKMapView` layer the flagship map requires.

---

## 4. B2B product — sell the roll-ups the map already computes

Build the aggregation engine once; the consumer maps and the sellable reports are the same pipeline at different privacy gates. Stand this up early — recurring contracted B2B revenue is the single biggest documented multiple-expansion lever at exit; you want to sell an **asset plus a revenue stream.**

- **Tier 1 — Regional Trend Reports** (self-serve dashboard + scheduled exports): style-share, momentum, sentiment by DMA/metro/state on rolling windows. Headline signals like "West Coast IPA check-in share +X% QoQ in Denver."
- **Tier 2 — Competitive & Distribution Intelligence:** over/under-index vs category and named competitors by region; "white-space" maps (high demand, low local supply) for taproom-siting and distribution.
- **Tier 3 — Custom / Clean-Room:** privacy-safe matching of our demand layer against a distributor's depletion or a brand's shipment data inside a data clean room — closed-loop "does regional buzz predict sell-through," no raw PII exposed.

Named, citable indices (always published with sample size *n* and window; sub-threshold cells suppressed): **Style Demand Index** (local share indexed to national = 100), **Momentum** (period-over-period Δ), **Flavor Sentiment Score** (ABSA net positivity per attribute per region), **Local Favorite Rank** (rating × local velocity, PII-safe).

Go dense in **2–4 launch metros** rather than thin nationally — a statistically meaningful single-market panel is more acquirable than shallow national breadth, and it de-risks the buyer's use case. Frame outputs as directional momentum/sentiment (not absolute market share): check-in users skew toward enthusiasts and hype styles, so calibrate against external anchors (Brewers Association production, distributor depletion) in clean-room engagements.

---

## 5. Privacy / consent architecture — the gating asset

The **company sale** (M&A) is the clean path; the **ongoing data sale** is the high-risk one. Both are enabled by the same day-one disclosures. The existential risk is the **FTC** (not the App Store or CCPA): an alcohol app selling precise location sits squarely in the 2024–2026 location-data-broker enforcement lane, and the FTC has *banned* companies from selling sensitive location outright.

**Legal stack shipped at launch (counsel-drafted):**
1. **Privacy Policy business-transfer clause** — personal data may be transferred as a business asset in a merger, acquisition, financing, reorganization, bankruptcy, or asset sale; the successor may continue processing under this policy.
2. **ToS assignment clause** — we may assign the agreement to a successor/acquirer without user consent.
3. **Dual disclosure** — data may be *sold/shared* with third parties for stated commercial purposes **and** *transferred* as an M&A asset. This dual disclosure is what keeps the company sale inside the CCPA business-transfer exemption.
4. **Purpose language** broad enough to cover aggregated/anonymized third-party analytics + data-clean-room use.
5. **Never** an absolute "we will never sell or transfer your data" line — it legally forecloses the exit and cannot be fixed retroactively on a churned user base.

**Consent capture (layered, unbundled, default-OFF):** neutral pre-permission explainer → iOS **When-In-Use** location prompt (request **Always** only with a genuine background feature) → a *separate* opt-in toggle for "share/sell my data with partners" and another for "use my precise location for [named purpose]" → the ATT system prompt with an honest `NSUserTrackingUsageDescription`. Selling to data brokers **is** "tracking" under Apple's definition, so ATT is mandatory; never gate features on ATT or bribe consent (5.1.2(i)).

**Durable consent + opt-out ledger (day one):** timestamped, versioned, per-purpose, storing the exact UI text shown, **exportable** to a buyer, including every CCPA do-not-sell/do-not-share and honored GPC signal. Under **AB 1824 (in force Jan 1 2025)** an acquirer inherits and must honor every pre-close opt-out (penalties $2,500–$7,500/violation) — a clean, exportable ledger is a diligence pass/fail that directly moves the price. (Model it append-only, mirroring the existing CLA-gate/provenance pattern already in the schema.)

**Apple compliance:** age-rate **18+** under the 2025 bands (the old 17+ no longer exists) + first-launch DOB gate (keep the DOB **out** of the saleable dataset). File Privacy Nutrition Labels declaring Location + Preferences/Purchases under **Data Linked to You** *and* **Data Used to Track You**; ship a `PrivacyInfo.xcprivacy` manifest (data types, tracking domains, required-reason APIs); audit every third-party SDK (their tracking is our violation).

**U.S. state law:** publish "Do Not Sell or Share" + "Limit the Use of My Sensitive PI" links, honor **GPC** automatically, classify precise geolocation as SPI, and **assume you are a California data broker** — calendar the Jan 1–31 CPPA registration (~$6,600), wire DROP bulk-deletion (live Aug 1 2026, 45-day cycle), and register in TX/OR/VT too. Run the CCPA risk assessment (required before selling/sharing or processing SPI as of Jan 1 2026).

**GDPR / EU:** selling data and monetizing location need freely-given, unbundled, withdrawable **opt-in** — legitimate interest will not hold; no tracking walls; fire no non-essential SDK before consent. GDPR anonymization must be *irreversible* (a higher bar than CCPA de-identification), and coarse/pseudonymized location often still counts as personal data. **Keep the EU dataset architecturally separable** (`region_code` / `is_eu_user`) so it can be excluded from a data sale a buyer isn't GDPR-comfortable with.

**Default commercial product = aggregated, anonymized layer via reports/dashboards/clean rooms, never raw PII** — the Untappd/Vivino model. It minimizes "sale of personal information" exposure, carries the highest margin, and is the format acquirers actually want.

---

## 6. Who buys, and what to track for the exit

**Three real acquirer classes, all with live rationales:** (1) vertical roll-ups / strategic operators — Next Glass bought Untappd (2016) + BeerAdvocate assets (2020), then took a Providence Strategic Growth investment; the thesis is owning the demand-side dataset + the B2B analytics product. (2) Big Beer / CPG for first-party data — AB InBev's BEES did $52.5B GMV in 2025 with a CDP holding ~100M records; brewery analytics is now competitive weaponry. (3) Loyalty / payments / martech — Mastercard bought SessionM (2019) to add a consumer-engagement data layer. A market-research buyer (Circana / NIQ) is plausible for the complementary attitudinal/occasion layer scanner data can't produce.

**M&A-readiness scorecard, tracked quarterly** (the numbers a Next-Glass- or Circana-type buyer underwrites): (1) check-ins/day + 90-day retention; (2) per-market density in target metros; (3) B2B ARR + logo count; (4) refresh velocity (data decays ~22–28%/yr); (5) consent-ledger completeness (an AB 1824 diligence pass/fail).
